develop_reconciliation.tenant_model_finding: AUTHORITATIVE develop connection model post-merge contradicts the pre-merge plan default of (tenant, org, product, configName). Evidence: (1) pkg/model/connection.go Connection struct has ProductName (required attribute) but NO OrganizationID and NO TenantID field. (2) The product ENTITY/table was removed (commit a6b8339, 'make connections standalone'); ProductName survives only as a string label. (3) pkg/mongodb/connection/indexes.go: unique index on config_name ALONE (partial deleted_at==nil), per-database. (4) pkg/mongodb/connection/connection.mongodb.go FindByName filters by config_name + deleted_at ONLY — no product/org/tenant filter. Tenant isolation is PHYSICAL (DB-per-tenant via tmcore), not a field. => Effective connection identity = (tenant_DB, config_name). Product is non-scoping; org absent. The Engine (in-process, cannot do DB-per-tenant) must carry tenantId logically. B2 scope-key decision escalated to owner: this finding contradicts both the plan default and the owner's stated 'tenantId > orgs/products' hierarchy.
develop_reconciliation.b2_decision: RESOLVED by owner 2026-06-01: drop org AND product from the Engine entirely. Rationale: the Engine is embedded INTO each product (matcher, reporter, ...), so the product IS the host process — it does not need to identify itself as 'product' inside its own embedded Engine, and org is the embedder's business grouping, not an Engine isolation concern. One embedded product may serve N tenants, so tenantId REMAINS the isolation boundary (owner: 'tenant TEM que estar no contexto', non-negotiable). DECISION: TenantContext = {TenantID, RequestID} (remove OrganizationID and ProductName); connection scope key / uniqueness = (tenantId, configName). This SIMPLIFIES the T-002/T-003 surface (fewer scope dimensions). Owner's 'tenantId > orgs/products' hierarchy still holds at the business layer — orgs/products live ABOVE the Engine in the host/product layer; the Engine sits below the product. CARRY-FORWARD for T-008/T-010: when wiring the legacy Manager onto the Engine, decide the disposition of the Manager's required ProductName field + X-Product-Name header (keep as host display attribute the Engine ignores, or remove).
